scaled_oracle_cases.description: Transformed seed case that verifies the function returns a list of 12 numeric values with specific endpoints, and that each call returns a fresh independent list.
scaled_oracle_cases.semantic_intent: The solution must return a list of 12 non-negative numeric values (first=1, last=0.0005), and each call returns a new independent list.
scaled_oracle_cases.target_constraint: Return a list of 12 numeric values, all non-negative, with first element exactly 1 and last element exactly 0.0005; each invocation returns a distinct list object (no shared reference).
scaled_oracle_cases.call_code:
profile = get_default_error_profile()
print("Default Illumina error profile:", profile)
assert isinstance(profile, list), "Profile should be a list"
assert len(profile) == 12, "Profile should contain 12 entries"
assert profile[0] == 1, "First entry should be 1"
assert profile[-1] == 0.0005, "Last entry should be 0.0005"
with open("error_profile.json", "w") as f:
    json.dump(profile, f)
print("Profile saved successfully to error_profile.json")
result = profile
scaled_oracle_cases.assertion_code:
# Additional scaled constraints
assert all(isinstance(v, (int, float)) for v in result), "All entries must be numeric"
assert all(v >= 0 for v in result), "All entries must be non-negative"
# Check function returns a new list each call
another_profile = get_default_error_profile()
assert another_profile is not result, "Function must return a new list each call"
# Check that modifying a different returned list does not affect result
yet_another = get_default_error_profile()
yet_another[0] = 999
assert result[0] == 1, "Original profile should remain unchanged"
covered_requirements: Scaled executable cases must expose the new input/data complexity introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new computation and contract constraints introduced by this operator.
Scaled executable cases must expose the new anti-shortcut robustness requirement introduced by this operator.
